title: lathyrus occidentalis subsp. hispanicus
intro: Lathyrus occidentalis subsp. hispanicus, also known as the Western sweet pea or Spanish vetchling, is a plant species in the pea family (Fabaceae). It has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly its seeds, which contain a compound called Lathyrine, a toxic amino acid that has been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and act as an antioxidant. Traditionally, the seeds of Lathyrus occidentalis subsp. hispanicus are used in herbal preparations, such as infusions and decoctions, to treat conditions like fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues.
